business.headline: SK Hynix surpasses Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company amid AI chip boomSK Hynix has emerged as one of the biggest beneficiaries thanks to its leadership in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, a key component used in advanced AI systems.

Elon Musk no longer a trillionaire as SpaceX, Tesla shares declineThe decline in Musk's fortune came after investors sold technology stocks amid concerns about an artificial intelligence-driven market bubble and the possibility of higher interest rates.

From inspections to intelligent compliance: The next step in implementing the ESI ActIn the final part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines why expanding social-security coverage is only half the battle, and how risk-based inspections, data analytics and AI-driven compliance could help ensure that workers actually receive the protections guaranteed under the ESI Act.

Why the Calcutta Stock Exchange has been shut for over a decadeIndia's oldest regional stock exchange has not seen a single trade since 2013. With West Bengal now proposing to revive it, here is exactly why it shut down in the first place.

Sensex ends 790 points higher, Nifty tops 24,000; IndiGo up 5%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 790.54 points, or 1.04%, to close at 76,991.22, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 197.55 points, or 0.83%, to settle at 24,021.65.

What is WhatsApp's new chief Kunal Shah's net worth?From launching CRED to building one of India's best-known fintech firms, Kunal Shah has come a long way. But how much wealth has he accumulated along the journey?

From HRA to NPS: 6 reasons old tax regime still helps you save more taxThe old tax regime may no longer be the default option, but it still offers several powerful tax-saving benefits. Could these six deductions help lower your tax bill more than the new regime?

CRED built a premium user base. Is that why Meta is paying $900 million?Meta is set to invest $900 million in CRED for a reported 20% stake. The deal points to the value of CRED's affluent users as much as its fintech stack.

How Noida's new airport redefines Uttar Pradesh's economic geographyThe start of flights at Noida International Airport marks a new phase for Jewar in UP. Its cargo-led, multi-modal design could reshape exports, industry and regional connectivity across North India.

Did India's compliance push cost it the next investment boom?India has improved ease of doing business over the past decade, but is that enough in a world increasingly shaped by AI, semiconductors and deep technology?

RBI not preparing for interest rate hikes yet, Governor Malhotra clarifiesMalhotra said the RBI is keeping an eye on whether higher oil prices start affecting the prices of other goods and services across the economy. According to him, such broader inflationary pressure has not appeared so far.

Sensex jumps nearly 800 points: Why is stock market rising today?Stock markets rebounded sharply on Wednesday as banking and IT shares led gains after the previous session's sell-off. Lower crude prices and supportive RBI signals lifted sentiment, even as monsoon and US rate risks remained.

Adani AGM: India's future cannot wait, says Gautam Adani on growth roadmapSpeaking at the Adani AGM, Chairman Gautam Adani said the group remained focused on nation-building despite facing challenges over the past year.

IRFC shares fall 5% today. Here's why the railway PSU stock is under pressureIRFC share price: The stock was trading at Rs 93.46 around 9:49 am, down over 5% for the day. The decline adds to the pressure on the PSU stock, which has fallen nearly 7% over the past month, 23% in six months and more than 32% over the past year.

Markets open flat as crude slump offsets hawkish Fed concernsBenchmark indices opened cautiously on Wednesday, with lower crude prices and a rebound in IT stocks aiding stability after the previous session's sell-off. Investors remained watchful of the monsoon, foreign flows and the US Federal Reserve as mixed cues kept sentiment restrained.

8th Pay Commission: OPS demand pending, but govt gives relief to these employeesThe government has said compassionate appointment cases filed by December 31, 2003, can be considered under the old pension rules even if the appointment came later. The clarification offers relief to affected employees while the wider OPS debate continues before the 8th Pay Commission.

Crude oil hits 4-month low. Will petrol and diesel prices fall?Crude oil prices fell to a near four-month low as Middle East tensions eased and supply prospects improved. The drop may help India's import bill, but petrol and diesel prices are unlikely to fall immediately.

Why many Indians earning Rs 1 lakh still struggle to saveFor many Indians, earning Rs 1 lakh a month once symbolised financial success. Yet, despite reaching this milestone, many professionals find themselves living pay cheque to pay cheque, with little left for savings. So where does all the money go?

Sebi looks to widen direct trading access; retail investors could benefitSebi has proposed allowing all categories of investors, including retail participants, to use Direct Market Access (DMA), a facility that enables orders to be placed directly into a stock exchange's trading system through a broker's infrastructure.

Sensex ends 893 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; Infosys falls 3%The S&P BSE Sensex dropped 893.39 points, or 1.16%, to close at 76,200.68, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 278.80 points, or 1.16%, to settle at 23,824.10.

entertainment.headline: Mammootty breaks silence on film body crisis after Shwetha Menon's resignation Days after AMMA's executive committee resigned, actor Mammootty has finally addressed the controversy. The actor described the ongoing crisis as a 'small matter' and said all issues would eventually be resolved.

Raakh's 'Babu' Akash Makhija says fans said his eyes reminded them of Irrfan Akash Makhija has spoken about the response to his performance as Babu in Raakh. He said the role ended years of waiting while also bringing intense reactions and fresh hope for work.

Bobby Deol is clueless about Latent 2, dodges question on Samay Raina's show A viral clip shows actor Bobby Deol responding to a question about India's Got Latent Season 2 during Alpha promotions. He reacted to actors Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's appearance on the show.

Alka Yagnik seen in wheelchair after Padma honour, video sparks concern A video of Alka Yagnik being assisted in a wheelchair surfaced online after her Padma Bhushan appearance. The clip drew concern from fans as the singer spoke about her health struggles and gradual return.
The Devil Wears Prada 2 OTT: When and where to watch the chic fashion-drama The doors of Runway Magazine are opening once again. The Devil Wears Prada 2 is finally making its way to OTT.

Vijay Deverakonda-Arnold Vosloo's face-off is Ranabaali's biggest moment: Director Director Rahul Sankrityan revealed that Ranabaali's climactic showdown between Vijay Deverakonda and The Mummy star Arnold Vosloo took 16 days to shoot and is a major highlight of the film.

Dr Armaan is back: Karan Singh Grover's Gram Chikitsalay 2 promo sparks nostalgia Karan Singh Grover appears as Dr Armaan Malik in the new Gram Chikitsalay 2 promo and surprises Dr Prabhat and Dr Gargi. The cameo has stirred nostalgia among Dill Mill Gayye fans.

'Peddi Nanna' Ram Charan says Peddi changed the way daughter Klin Kaara sees him Actor Ram Charan, at the success meet of Peddi, shared how the film changed the way his daughter addresses him. He said the response feels unlike any earlier hit and thanked the team behind the film.

What exactly is Rajinikanth playing in Dharman? Director finally drops biggest clue At the launch of Rajinikanth's Dharman, director Ashwath Marimuthu said the film is a family-oriented thriller, not a "5 Rupees Doctor" story. His remarks, along with the first-look poster and production update, sharpened interest in the superstar's role as shooting begins on Thursday.

Darshan approaches Supreme Court over fresh bail plea in Renukaswamy murder case Darshan has asked the Supreme Court to clarify that he can seek fresh bail if the Renukaswamy case trial continues to crawl. The plea says the earlier order was meant as a safeguard, not a bar on relief amid delay or hardship.

Hina Khan reacts to Pune fort murder: Could have spared him with one truth Hina Khan reacted to the Ketan Agarwal case after police said his fiancee and her alleged lover plotted his death at Lohagad Fort.

What keeps Mollywood's Balan: The Boy from becoming a perfect film Balan: The Boy has drawn acclaim for its performances, atmosphere and precise observations. The debate now is why its unresolved motivations and thinner supporting arcs keep it from greatness.

Dharman first look X reactions: Fans celebrate Rajinikanth's mass fest Rajinikanth's first look from Dharman has sparked excitement online, with fans praising his intense avatar. Impressed fans called the title reveal 'pakka mass' and appreciated the superstar's deadly new role.

Guddu, Kaleen, Munna bhaiya promise mayhem in Mirzapur The Movie posters The makers of Mirzapur: The Movie have unveiled first-look posters of Kaleen Bhaiya, Guddu Bhaiya and Munna Bhaiya ahead of the teaser launch. The film will release in theatres on September 4.

The Sting Within Me: DJ Alan Walker on making Formula 1 music, Indian fans and risks Norwegian music producer and DJ Alan Walker opened up about his Formula 1-inspired track, The Sting Within Me. In an exclusive interview, he also spoke about his love for India, creative risks, AI in music, and the story behind his iconic hoodie and mask.

Director Curry Barker can't believe Obsession has earned Rs 78 crore in India Obsession director Curry Barker reacted to the horror-drama's Rs 78 crore run in India. He said the low-budget film's success and praise had taken him by surprise.

Film body seeks FIR against Bhansali, Rs 1 crore as worker dies on Love and War set Cine workers body has written to Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis seeking an FIR against Sanjay Leela Bhansali after crew member Chandradhari Singh Yadav died on the set of Love and War in Mumbai. The body has alleged safety lapses and sought Rs 1 crore compensation for the family.

Chiranjeevi recalls the day Ram Charan came close to losing eye during Peddi Chiranjeevi revealed that Ram Charan nearly lost an eye during Peddi's shoot and praised his son's dedication, calling the film one of the finest performances of his career. He made the statement during the recent success event of the film.

Pralay loading? Ranveer Singh drops by Hansal Mehta's office with Mukesh Chhabra Actor Ranveer Singh was seen leaving producer Hansal Mehta's office in Bandra, Mumbai, with casting director Mukesh Chhabra. The meeting occurred amid growing speculation about his film Pralay and a possible announcement soon.

general news.headline: Delhi-Amritsar Air India flight briefly enters Pak airspace during go-aroundAn Air India flight from Delhi to Amritsar briefly entered Pakistani airspace near Attari for under two minutes during a go-around on June 22 before returning to Delhi.

Passport is a travel document, not document of citizenship: GovtThe Ministry of External Affairs said a passport is not a document that establishes citizenship, even though it is issued only to Indian citizens. The clarification, made on Passport Seva Divas, has renewed debate over what counts as conclusive proof of citizenship.

Cried all night, Suvendu stood by me: Mahua Moitra's praise for CM fuels buzzMahua Moitra, among Mamata Banerjee's shrinking band of loyalists, said she shared an emotional connection with Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari. Her remarks have sparked fresh speculation as the Trinamool faces defections and unrest.

Mossad plotted to kill Munir during Geneva talks: Brazilian journalist's big claimBrazilian journalist and geopolitical expert Pepe Escobar alleged that Israeli intelligence agency Mossad plotted to eliminate Pakistani military chief Asim Munir and the country's delegation in Geneva during Iran talks. He said Pakistan, after getting to know of the plot, threatened to "wipe Israel off the map". Escobar's claims were rejected by Pakistani journalists.

Bangladesh to buy Chinese jets used by Pak in Op Sindoor. What it means for IndiaBangladesh's reported plan to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE fighter jets, the aircraft used by Pakistan against India during Operation Sindoor, marks a major step in its military modernisation drive. While the deal might dramatically enhance Dhaka's air combat capabilities, it could also deepen China's influence even in India's eastern neighbourhood.

4 dead, 15 trapped after Kolkata warehouse collapses, Army joins rescue opsMassive iron beams and concrete slabs of a Kolkata under-construction building came crashing down as workers were engaged in construction activity, trapping several people under the rubble.

I grew up...: Usha Vance on why she never converted to Christianity like JD VanceUsha Vance opens up about religion, marriage and conversion rumours as JD Vance's faith journey returns to the spotlight.

Mumbai local passenger stabbed to death after row over closing door, accused heldA row over shutting a train door during heavy rain allegedly ended with a commuter stabbing Mayank Ramesh Lohar on a Mumbai local. Police have launched a manhunt and are reviewing CCTV and witness accounts to piece together the sequence.

The AI shortcut problem: Why some 8.5+ CGPA engineering grads struggle with basicsA viral post by a MAANG engineer has reignited a growing debate in tech hiring. As AI tools become part of everyday coding, employers, educators and students are confronting a difficult question: are graduates learning faster, or are they skipping the fundamentals that make great engineers?

Pak an outlier among Muslim countries, saw support for US grow amid Iran warA Pew Research Center study has revealed that Pakistan is the only Muslim-majority country where favourable views of the US increased even as war raged on in Iran. The fieldwork in Pakistan began just days before Islamabad hosted the first round of US-Iran peace talks, suggesting that the country's role as a mediator might have contributed to the shift in public opinion.

Total surrender: Police face storm as Nihangs ride away after gurdwara standoffAfter a three-day standoff, the Nihangs who had occupied Uttarakhand's Nagrasu Gurdwara vacated it after talks with local administration, including the police. But their departure on motorcycles, and lack of punitive action, have sparked a wave of criticism.

Kerala CM smells of Bacardi: What's the liquor storm targeting VD SatheesanA proposed tax cut on low-alcohol beverages has snowballed into Kerala Chief Minister VD Satheesan's first major political crisis.

Jamun trees are loaded like never before: Does it hint at a coming drought?Indian markets are overflowing with jamun this summer and a viral thread linked it to a grandmother's warning about drought. The science is more nuanced than that.

How Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram put woman-led Telugu cinema back in spotlightSamantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram has opened strongly as a female-led Telugu commercial film. Its promising box office run and overseas pull have sharpened focus on changing audience support for women-led stories.

Why Lohagad? The fort Siya chose to kill her fiancéEach event leading up to Ketan Agarwal's death appears to circle back to Lohagad. But what made this rugged hill fort the alleged choice for the murder?

A million AI satellites in orbit? Elon Musk says Starmind is comingElon Musk has confirmed that "Starmind" will be the name of SpaceX's proposed AI satellite constellation. The project aims to place AI-powered computing infrastructure in orbit, with reports suggesting the network could eventually include up to one million satellites.

BJP in, TMC out, Kolkata to join Delhi NCR, Mumbai in race for airportsThe BJP-led government in West Bengal has announced plans for a greenfield airport near Kalyani, around 50 km north of Kolkata. Once operational, it will make Kolkata India's third metropolitan region with multiple commercial airports. Mamata Banerjee, as the CM, had resisted the Centre's plan for a second airport.

Flooded roads, uprooted trees, wall collapse: Monsoon hits Mumbai hard Live UpdatesHeavy rain left key parts of Mumbai, including Dadar station, waterlogged after several areas of the city recorded more than 300 mm of rainfall over the past 24 hours. The rainfall falls in the IMD's "extremely heavy" category, highlighting the intensity of the monsoon downpour.

Hoodie in June heat, phone left at office: How cops cracked Pune fort murderA CCTV camera installed near the fort's ticket counter captured a hooded man, later identified as Chetan Chaudhary, on June 18 trailing Pune businessman Ketan Agarwal and his fiancée Siya Goyal. Just hours later, Ketan was dead.

Elon Musk no longer a trillionaire as SpaceX, Tesla shares declineThe decline in Musk's fortune came after investors sold technology stocks amid concerns about an artificial intelligence-driven market bubble and the possibility of higher interest rates.

Delhi BJP MLA Kailash Gahlot's daughter Devina tops CUET UG 2026, secures AIR 1Securing AIR 1 in CUET UG 2026, Delhi's former minister Kailash Gahlot's daughter said regular revision, conceptual clarity from NCERT and self-belief were central to her preparation strategy.

Bill Gates reveals 3 affairs, blackmail fears in bombshell Epstein testimonyUS lawmakers sought Bill Gates' testimony after the release of additional documents by the Justice Department renewed scrutiny over his past interactions with the disgraced financier, who died in a jail cell in 2019.

How a gamcha helped police dog Mary trace rape accused to his house in UPA trained police dog named Mary helped Sambhal Police trace the accused in the sexual assault case of a six-year-old girl. Her scent trail from a recovered gamcha gave investigators the lead they needed after initial inquiries stalled.

Iran told US no Hormuz tolls, talks will end if details are untrue: TrumpDonald Trump said Iran had assured the United States that ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz would not face tolls, insurance fees or any other charges.

sports.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reacts to brother's impressive knock: Hundreds run in blood Ashirvad Sooryavanshi made 168 off 119 balls in a local match, with Vaibhav sharing the scorecard online. The innings deepened talk of another Sooryavanshi batting talent emerging from Bihar.

Amid terrible batting form, Harmanpreet Kaur finds advice from ex-teammate India captain Harmanpreet Kaur's batting form has become a growing concern at the Women's T20 World Cup, but former teammate Shikha Pandey believes the solution lies in returning to her natural game.

Too young for dressing room? Sooryavanshi's UK tour comes with a twist Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will use separate changing facilities during India's England tour under ICC safeguarding rules. The arrangement shows how cricket's child-protection systems are adapting to a 15-year-old entering the senior game.

Chasing like Dhoni Book Review: Story of the other India Ayush Purthan and Samod's Chasing Like Dhoni examines India's grassroots cricket world through the shift triggered by MS Dhoni's rise. The book shows how that dream widened beyond big cities while exposing its emotional, financial and structural costs.

Shubman Gill surpasses Kohli in ODI rankings, Root reigns supreme in Tests India ODI captain Shubman Gill has climbed to the second spot in the ODI batting rankings. Meanwhile, Joe Root, who became only the 2nd batter to reach 14,000 Test runs, has regained his place as the No.1 Test batter in the world.

Divided by fans, united by stats: Two GOATS, uncanny similarities FIFA World Cup 2026: Ronaldo silenced retirement calls with a record-setting brace as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0. His reply dragged Messi back into frame, with both greats now sharing an identical 20-year, 11-day World Cup scoring span.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reminds Kapil Dev of 16-year-old Sachin Tendulkar India's first World Cup-winning captain, Kapil Dev has compared Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to a young Sachin Tendulkar, but said it is far too early to judge the teenager's career.

Axed from Asian Games, Manika Batra threatens to sue table tennis association One of India's top table tennis players, Manika Batra, has threatened legal action against the Table Tennis Federation of India (TTFI). Batra, who was left out of India's Asian Games squad, has demanded transparency from the federation over the selection process.

Stokes is a good friend: McCullum dismisses talk of fallout with England captain England coach Brendon McCullum dismissed speculation of a fallout with captain Ben Stokes, insisting the pair remain "good friends" and have "no idea" where the rumours originated.

Ghana witch doctor strikes? Harry Kane fails to score in England's World Cup draw Ghanaian fans have been celebrating the role of self-styled witch doctor Nana Kwaku Bonsam after Harry Kane endured a frustrating evening in England's goalless draw against Ghana at the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Congo superfan finally arrives in USA, aura farms at FIFA World Cup DR Congo super fans Michel Kuka Mboladinga finally arrived at the FIFA World Cup 2026 on Wednesday, June 24. Standing tall in the team's match against Colombia, Mboladinga was a source of inspiration for the team.

Daniel Munoz sends Colombia into knockouts with narrow win over DR CongoDaniel Munoz's second-half goal finally broke DR Congo's resistance as Colombia secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory in Guadalajara on Tuesday to book a place in the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Jeremy Doku returns to Belgium squad after son's birthJeremy Doku is set to rejoin Belgium after leaving camp for the birth of his son. His absence sparked debate, but teammates backed him before a crucial match against New Zealand.

Neymar fit and available but not certain to play vs Scotland, says AncelottiNeymar is available for Brazil's final Group C clash against Scotland at the FIFA World Cup, but coach Carlo Ancelotti remained tight-lipped on whether the forward will feature in Miami after recovering from a calf injury.

Cristiano Ronaldo, scrutiny's favourite child, answers with grace and a braceCristiano Ronaldo has spent 23 years turning doubt into fuel, and Houston offered another chapter in that story. Written off after DR Congo, the Portugal captain responded with a brace, a record and a reminder that criticism remains his favourite opponent.

Brave Panama's World Cup fight ends as Croatia stays alive with 1-0 winAnte Budimir's second-half header gave Croatia a 1-0 win over Panama in Toronto. The result kept Croatia alive in Group L and eliminated Panama from the FIFA World Cup 2026.

I don't deserve it: Jude Bellingham's blunt take on MOTM award after Ghana drawJude Bellingham admitted he did not deserve the Man of the Match award after England's attacking struggles were exposed in a frustrating 0-0 draw against Ghana in Group L in Boston.

All-round Perry gets Australia ready for India tie with Pakistan hammeringEllyse Perry's 71 and 2/9 drove Australia to a 113-run win over Pakistan in Leeds. The result left Australia on the verge of the semi-finals before a high-stakes meeting with India at Lord's.

Second game syndrome hits England hard in lacklustre draw against GhanaEngland were held to a goalless draw by Ghana in Boston after failing to break down a deep defence. The result revived a familiar second-match problem for the Three Lions and raised questions on Thomas Tuchel's tactics in the contest.

France coach Didier Deschamps to miss Norway clash after mother's deathFIFA World Cup 2026: France coach Didier Deschamps has returned home after the death of his mother and will miss Friday's World Cup match against Norway. Assistant Guy Stephan will take charge as France, already through, look to finish top of Group I.

Iran catch a break: US relaxes World Cup travel rules before Egypt clashFIFA World Cup 2026: The United States has allowed Iran to enter Seattle two days before its World Cup match against Egypt. The change gives Team Melli more preparation time, though post-match exit rules remain in place.

Cristiano Ronaldo ignores reporter's Messi question after Portugal brace. VideoFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking night for Portugal took an unexpected turn when he appeared to ignore a question about Lionel Messi. The brief interaction quickly went viral, reigniting discussion around football's most iconic rivalry.

3 injured in stampede outside Ranchi stadium during Jharkhand T20 LeagueA crowd surge at the West Gate before the Jharkhand T20 League final left spectators injured at Ranchi's JSCA Stadium on Tuesday, June 23.

Written off again, Cristiano Ronaldo answers with a brace as Portugal hit fiveFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo arrived in Houston surrounded by questions about age, relevance and whether Portugal had outgrown him. Ninety minutes later, he left with a brace, another World Cup record and a reminder that doubting him remains football's favourite mistake.

COL 1-0 COD highlights: Colombia beat DR Congo to reach knockoutsColombia (COL) vs DR Congo (COD) highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Colombia booked their place in the knockout stage of the 2026 World Cup with a hard-fought 1-0 win over DR Congo in Guadalajara. The deadlock was finally broken in the 76th minute when Daniel Munoz found the net, ending the resistance of goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi, who had produced a series of fine saves to keep the Africans in the contest.

Cristiano Ronaldo silences critics, becomes 1st player to score in 6 World CupsCristiano Ronaldo scored twice against Uzbekistan in Houston as Portugal took control. His opener made him the first player to score in six World Cups and answered criticism after the draw with DR Congo.

3,784 days of dominance: India beat Pakistan in 7-goal London hockey thrillerIndia edged Pakistan 4-3 in a chaotic FIH Pro League clash in London. The win stretched India's unbeaten run against Pakistan to 3,784 days despite a frantic late fightback.

Tejaswin Shankar gets AFI exemption, stays in Asian Games selection raceAFI has granted Tejaswin Shankar exemption from the National Inter-State Championships and will still consider him for the Asian Games. The decision acknowledges the recovery demands of the decathlon and keeps the national record holder in contention.

POR vs UZB Highlights: Undeniable Ronaldo, dominant Portugal hit 5 in routPortugal (POR) vs Uzbekistan (UZB) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo turned the spotlight into a stage, scoring twice as Portugal dismantled Uzbekistan in Houston. With Bruno Fernandes pulling the strings and set-pieces doing the damage, Roberto Martinez's side have looked every bit the contenders they promised to be.

New Zealand survive Scotland scare, remain alive in semis raceWomen's T20 Wolrd CUp 2026: Izzy Sharp and Brooke Halliday revived New Zealand to chase 132 after an early wobble. Darcey Carter's unbeaten 72 proved insufficient as Scotland's semi-final hopes ended, keeping New Zealand alive.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
